title: Kaitiakitanga in Action: Guardians of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream / Te Mahi Kaitiaki: Ngā Kaitiaki o Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
subject: Social Studies
culturalContext: This lesson is deeply rooted in our local awa (stream), Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, which flows through our rohe. Students will explore the historical significance of this waterway as a traditional mahinga kai (food gathering place) for Ngāti Kahungunu, its role in local pūrākau (legends), and current environmental challenges facing our community. The learning connects to the story of Haumiatiketike, the atua of uncultivated food, and the concept of mauri - the life force of our waterways.
learningObjectives: Investigate and document three historical uses of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku by Ngāti Kahungunu through interviews with local kaumatua
Analyze current water quality data from three testing sites along the awa using scientific measurement tools and traditional observation methods
Develop and present a kaitiakitanga action plan proposing specific measures to address one identified environmental issue affecting the awa
Collaboratively create a digital storymap that layers historical significance, current environmental data, and proposed guardianship actions for the awa

activities.title: Whakapapa of the Awa: Connecting to Our Waterway
activities.description: Students visit three significant sites along Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku with local kaumatua to hear pūrākau and learn about traditional uses as mahinga kai. They create annotated sketches and record oral histories about the cultural significance of each site.

activities.title: Mauri Meter: Assessing the Life Force of Our Awa
activities.description: Working with local environmental scientists, students conduct water quality testing at three sites using pH meters, turbidity tubes, and macroinvertebrate surveys. They learn to assess mauri through both scientific measurements and traditional observation methods (clarity, smell, presence of life).

activities.title: Voices of the Awa: Community Perspectives
activities.description: Students interview diverse community members (farmers, local iwi representatives, council environmental officers) about their perspectives on the awa's health and management. They practice active listening and develop empathy for different viewpoints.

activities.title: Kaitiakitanga in Action: Designing Solutions
activities.description: Using design thinking principles, student groups develop practical kaitiakitanga projects addressing one identified issue (e.g., planting day for bank stabilization, community education campaign about stormwater pollution, design for riparian planting)

activities.title: Digital Storymap Creation
activities.description: Students create layered digital maps using ArcGIS or Google Earth that integrate historical significance, current environmental data, interview insights, and their proposed kaitiakitanga solutions for presentation to the community.

resources: Local kaumatua from Ngāti Kahungunu (specifically Aunty Hine Smith and Uncle Wiremu Brown)
Hawke's Bay Regional Council's 'State of the Environment' monitoring data and water testing kits
The book 'Ngāti Kahungunu: The People and Their Traditions' by J. H. Mitchell
Digital mapping tools (ArcGIS Online educational licenses provided by the school)

tasks: Produce a detailed digital storymap showing historical, cultural, and environmental layers of the awa
Present a well-researched kaitiakitanga action plan to community stakeholders
Create a reflective journal documenting learning journey and personal connection to place
Participate in a practical kaitiakitanga activity (e.g., planting day, water testing)

nzcAlignment: Social Studies L4: Understand how people participate individually and collectively in response to community challenges
Social Studies L4: Understand how the ways in which leadership of groups is acquired and exercised have consequences for communities and societies
Social Studies L4: Understand how people's management of resources impacts on environmental and social sustainability
Health and PE L4: Demonstrate respect for others and develop a sense of social responsibility
